steps.text: The quadratic model is graphed with speed (mph) on the x-axis and stopping distance (feet) on the y-axis. Locate \(x = 55\) mph on the horizontal axis — it falls halfway between the 50 and 60 mph marks.
Trace vertically from \(x = 55\) up to the curve, then read the y-value. The curve at that point sits near the 210-foot mark, between the 200 and 220 grid lines.

Evaluate every choice:
  • A: 25 ft — Far too low; even at very low speeds the stopping distance would be near or above this. Eliminated.
  • B: 30 ft — Still far too low for 55 mph on this scale. Eliminated.
  • C: 210 ft — Consistent with reading the quadratic curve at \(x = 55\). That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: 250 ft — This is closer to the value at 60+ mph on the curve, not at 55 mph. Eliminated.
